zh.Scot's Hall, Kent, England: 英國肯特斯可也斯 霍爾
zh.lifeSummary: John le Scot I是William Balliol le Scot的兒子，於1290年出生在英國肯特斯可也斯 霍爾。他於1309年與Helena ferch Dafydd of Ashford在英國肯特布拉布恩結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1348年在英國肯特爾哈姆享年58歲。

zh-hans.Scot's Hall, Kent, England: 英国肯特斯可也斯 霍爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: John le Scot I是William Balliol le Scot的儿子，于1290年出生在英国肯特斯可也斯 霍爾。他于1309年与Helena ferch Dafydd of Ashford在英国肯特布拉布恩结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1348年在英国肯特爾哈姆享年58岁。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Scot
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Scott
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish (Down): habitational and ethnic name from Middle English Scot ‘man from Scotland’. There is no evidence that the surname denoted either of the earlier senses of Scot as ‘(Gaelic-speaking) Irishman’ or ‘man from Alba’, the Gaelic-speaking region of Scotland north of the river Forth. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
English and Scottish: from the rare Middle English personal name Scot (Old English Scott, possibly also Old Norse Skotr), only certainly attested in northern England.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: John le Scot I was born in 1290, in Scot's Hall, Kent, England as the son of William Balliol le Scot. He married Helena ferch Dafydd of Ashford in 1309, in Brabourne, Kent, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1348, in Chilham, Kent, England, at the age of 58.

fr.lifeSummary: John le Scot I est né en 1290, à Scot's Hall, Kent, Angleterre, fils de William Balliol le Scot. Il a épousé Helena ferch Dafydd of Ashford en 1309, à Brabourne, Kent, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1348, à Chilham, Kent, Angleterre, à l’âge de 58 ans.

de.lifeSummary: John le Scot I wurde 1290 in Scot's Hall, Kent, England, als Sohn von William Balliol le Scot geboren. Er hat 1309 in Brabourne, Kent, England Helena ferch Dafydd of Ashford geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1348 im Alter von 58 Jahren in Chilham, Kent, England.

it.lifeSummary: John le Scot I è nato il nel 1290 nel Scot's Hall, Kent, England come figlio di William Balliol le Scot. Lui sposato Helena ferch Dafydd of Ashford nel 1309, nel Brabourne, Kent, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1348, nel Chilham, Kent, England, all'età di 58 anni.

ja.Brabourne, Kent, England: イギリスケントブラボウルン
ja.lifeSummary: John le Scot Iは1290年にイギリスケントスコト'ス ハルでWilliam Balliol le Scotの息子として生まれた。1309年にイギリスケントブラボウルンでHelena ferch Dafydd of Ashfordと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1348年にイギリスケントチルハムで58歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: John le Scot I(은)는 1290년 영국 켄트 스컷'스 홀에서 William Balliol le Scot의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1309년 영국 켄트 브라보우른에서 Helena ferch Dafydd of Ashford(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1348년 영국 켄트 치함에서 58세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: John le Scot I nasceu em 1290, em Scot's Hall, Kent, Inglaterra filho de William Balliol le Scot. Ele casou-se com Helena ferch Dafydd of Ashford em 1309, em Brabourne, Kent, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 1348, em Chilham, Kent, Inglaterra, com 58 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: John le Scot I родился в 1290 г. в местности скот'с холл, кэнт, Англия. Его отца звали William Balliol le Scot. Он женился на женщине по имени Helena ferch Dafydd of Ashford в 1309 г. в местности брабурн, кэнт, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1348 г. в местности чилхам, кэнт, Англия в возрасте 58 лет.

es.lifeSummary: John le Scot I nació en 1290, en Scot's Hall, Kent, Inglaterra como hijo de William Balliol le Scot. Se casó con Helena ferch Dafydd of Ashford en 1309, en Brabourne, Kent, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1348, en Chilham, Kent, Inglaterra, a la edad de 58 años.
